notes_to_memory: The gated-local search now appears exhausted, so this round shifts to the routed family as the most meaningful architecture alternative presently available.
Routed should now provide a genuinely different support-selection behavior from plain top-k and gated, making this proxy result informative even if it does not beat the frontier.
This run keeps the strongest validated training regime fixed so the readout focuses on the routed selection mechanism rather than confounded optimization changes.
next_hypotheses: If routed proxy FVU is competitive with or better than top-k and gated, keep the family alive and promote or stabilize it as the next main architecture path.
If routed runs cleanly but is clearly worse, conclude that this support-selection variant is not the missing ingredient and deprioritize it.
If routed quality is promising but throughput regresses, follow with a routed-specific performance pass rather than discarding the family on speed alone.
